business.name: Elite Flooring
business.serviceArea: Bainbridge Island & North Seattle — Ballard, Magnolia, Queen Anne, Fremont, Wallingford, Phinney Ridge, Green Lake
business.about: A flooring studio serving Bainbridge Island and the north Seattle neighborhoods. Material selection guidance, proper subfloor prep, dustless refinishing, and clean installs — every floor built to look right on day one and hold up through a decade of PNW life.

services.name: Hardwood Install
services.description: Solid and engineered hardwood installation with proper acclimation, subfloor flatness verified, nailed or glued to spec. Transitions, thresholds, and base shoe finished to match. Five-year labor warranty.

services.name: LVP & Laminate
services.description: Luxury vinyl plank and laminate floating installation — the right choice for PNW homes with moisture variability. Underlayment selected for your subfloor type, expansion gaps maintained.

services.name: Tile Install
services.description: Floor and wall tile in kitchens, bathrooms, mudrooms, and entries. Uncoupling membrane for crack isolation, grout sealed, and transition to adjacent flooring done clean.

services.name: Hardwood Refinishing
services.description: Dustless drum and edge sanding, stain color matching or change, and 3-coat water-based or oil-based polyurethane finish. Most floors returned to use within 24 hours of final coat.

services.name: Carpet
services.description: Carpet in bedrooms, stairs, and bonus rooms with pad selected for density and use. Seams planned to minimize visibility, edges tacked and trimmed clean.

services.name: Subfloor Repair
services.description: Squeaky floor remediation, OSB and plywood replacement, deflection correction, and self-leveling compound for floors that are out of flat. The work that makes the finish floor last.

testimonials.name: Diana C.
testimonials.text: Refinished our 1940s fir floors. Dustless system actually worked — no dust cloud in the house. The stain color they recommended is better than what I had originally planned.

testimonials.name: Alex B.
testimonials.text: LVP throughout our main floor. They fixed two soft spots in the subfloor before laying anything. The installer said those soft spots would have caused failures within a year otherwise.

testimonials.name: Melissa W.
testimonials.text: Tile work in two bathrooms. Grout lines are perfectly consistent and the transitions into the hardwood hallway are seamless. Detail work I didn't expect at this price point.

testimonials.name: Patrick O.
testimonials.text: Full hardwood install on the second floor. They acclimated the wood for five days before laying a single plank — old house, varying humidity. It's been 18 months and not a single gap or buckle.

process.title: Consult
process.description: We assess subfloor condition, moisture levels, and existing transitions, then help you choose the right material for your actual use case — not just what looks good in a showroom.

process.title: Prep
process.description: Subfloor flatness verified and corrected. Moisture tested. Any squeaks, soft spots, or height transitions addressed before the first plank drops.

process.title: Install
process.description: Clean, systematic installation with proper acclimation, expansion gaps, and transitions planned from day one. Furniture moved and replaced. Job site clean every evening.

process.title: Finish
process.description: Walk-through with the homeowner before we pack up. Final grout sealing, caulk at vertical transitions, and a five-year labor warranty on hardwood installs.

chatbot.greeting: Hi — Elite Flooring's consultant. Tell me what kind of flooring you're thinking about and I'll help you pick the right material and get a quote started.
qualifyingQuestions: What type of flooring are you considering — hardwood, LVP, tile, carpet, or refinishing?
Which rooms or what area in square feet?
Do you know what's under the existing floor — concrete slab or wood subfloor?
chatbot.customInstructions: Anchor on subfloor prep quality, material selection guidance for PNW moisture conditions, and the dustless refinishing system. Reference 5-year labor warranty on hardwood installs. Help users who are undecided between materials — this is a strength. Steer toward an in-home measure before quoting precise numbers.

persona.name: Olivia
persona.title: Flooring Consultant
persona.proactiveBubbleText: Hi — hardwood, LVP, tile, or refinishing? I can help you figure out the right fit.

faqs.question: What's the difference between solid hardwood and engineered?
faqs.answer: Solid hardwood can be refinished more times but is moisture-sensitive. Engineered hardwood handles PNW humidity variability better while still looking and feeling like real wood.

faqs.question: Is your refinishing actually dustless?
faqs.answer: Dustless drum and edge sanding — not zero dust, but 97%+ contained. Far better than traditional open-drum sanding.

faqs.question: How long does hardwood installation take?
faqs.answer: Most jobs are 2–5 days depending on area size. We add acclimation time if needed for solid hardwood — usually 3–5 days before install.

faqs.question: Do you fix subfloor problems before installing?
faqs.answer: Yes — always. Soft spots, squeaks, and out-of-flat subfloor are addressed before a single plank goes down.
